title: Project Initiation Document – 7 Simple Creation Steps Overview
overview: A concise list of the seven essential steps to create a Project Initiation Document, from providing context to sharing the final document.
detailed_summary: The article outlines a seven‑step process for building a Project Initiation Document tailored for Digital Project Management. The steps are: 1) Provide the Context, 2) Define the Project Parameters, 3) Define the Specifics, 4) Define the Project Breakdown Structure and Resourcing Plan, 5) Define Who’s Who (RACI), 6) Identify Risks, Assumptions, Issues, and Dependencies, and 7) Share the Project Initiation Document. Each step focuses on a distinct knowledge area, ensuring the PID is comprehensive yet not overly complex.
content:
7 Simple Steps To Create A Project Initiation Document
1. Provide The Context
2. Define The Project Parameters
3. Define The Specifics
4. Define the Project Breakdown Structure and Resourcing Plan
5. Define Who’s Who
6. Identify Your Risks, Assumptions, Issues And Dependencies
7. Share Your Project Initiation Document
